enonce.texte:
Match each food with the correct food group.

Foods: rice, apples, fish, milk, carrots

Food Groups: A. Fruits, B. Vegetables, C. Grains, D. Dairy, E. Meat/Protein

questions.texte: Match each food with its food group.
questions.reponse:
1. rice → C. Grains
2. apples → A. Fruits
3. fish → E. Meat/Protein
4. milk → D. Dairy
5. carrots → B. Vegetables

enonce.texte:
Read the steps for making a fruit salad. Put them in the correct order using sequence markers: First, Then, After that, Finally.

Steps:

  • Eat the fruit salad.
  • Wash the fruits.
  • Add some yogurt.
  • Cut the fruits into small pieces.

questions.texte: Order the steps using the correct sequence markers.
questions.reponse: First, wash the fruits. Then, cut the fruits into small pieces. After that, add some yogurt. Finally, eat the fruit salad.

enonce.texte:
Read the text carefully and answer the questions.

A Healthy Breakfast

Breakfast is the most important meal of the day. A balanced breakfast gives you energy for the whole morning. A good breakfast should include fruits, grains, and protein. For example, you can eat bread with eggs and drink some milk. You can also add a banana or an apple. First, prepare your food. Then, eat slowly. After that, drink some water. Finally, go to school with energy!

questions.texte: Answer the questions about the text.
questions.reponse:
1. Breakfast is the most important meal of the day.
2. A good breakfast should include fruits, grains, and protein.
3. You can eat bread with eggs and drink some milk.
4. You can add a banana or an apple.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the most important meal of the day according to the text?
What three food groups should a good breakfast include?
What food can you eat for a healthy breakfast? Give two examples.
sousQuestions.reponse: Bread with eggs and milk (or banana/apple)

enonce.texte: Unscramble the letters to form the correct food or meal vocabulary word.

You are responsible for planning a balanced menu for one day. Write a short paragraph (5-6 sentences) describing what you will eat for breakfast, lunch, and dinner.

Useful vocabulary: breakfast, lunch, dinner, fruits, vegetables, grains, protein, balanced, healthy

Use sequence markers: first, then, after that, finally

questions.texte: Write a paragraph describing your daily menu.
questions.reponse:
A good paragraph should include:
- Breakfast, lunch, and dinner mentioned
- At least one food from different food groups (fruits, vegetables, grains, protein)
- Sequence markers (first, then, after that, finally)
- 5-6 sentences
- Correct grammar and spelling

enonce.texte:
Read the dialogue and answer the questions.

Dialogue: At the Health Club

Paul: I want to eat healthy food. What should I eat?

Maria: You should eat fruits and vegetables every day.

Paul: What about meat?

Maria: You can eat fish and chicken. They are good sources of protein.

Paul: And what should I drink?

Maria: Drink plenty of water. Avoid sugary drinks.

Paul: Thank you, Maria. I will follow your advice.

questions.texte: Answer the questions about the dialogue.
questions.reponse:
1. Paul wants to eat healthy food.
2. Maria says Paul should eat fruits and vegetables every day.
3. Paul can eat fish and chicken.
4. Maria recommends drinking plenty of water and avoiding sugary drinks.

sousQuestions.reponse: He wants to eat healthy food.

sousQuestions.texte: What does Maria suggest Paul should eat every day?
What good sources of protein does Maria mention?
What should Paul drink according to Maria?

intitule: Critical Thinking: Is This Meal Healthy?

enonce.texte:
Read the meal description and answer the questions.

John's Dinner

John had a big dinner. He ate three pieces of fried chicken, a large portion of chips, a can of soda, and a slice of chocolate cake for dessert. He did not eat any vegetables. After dinner, he felt very full and tired.

questions.texte: Analyze John's dinner and answer the questions.
questions.reponse:
1. John ate fried chicken, chips, soda, and chocolate cake. He did not eat any vegetables.
2. The meal is unhealthy because it contains too much fat and sugar, and no vegetables.
3. A healthier alternative could be grilled chicken with salad and water, with fruit for dessert.

sousQuestions.texte: What did John eat for dinner?
sousQuestions.reponse: Fried chicken, chips, soda, and chocolate cake.

sousQuestions.texte: Do you think John's dinner is healthy? Why or why not?
sousQuestions.reponse: No, it's not healthy because it has too much fat and sugar, and no vegetables.

sousQuestions.texte: What could John eat instead to make his dinner healthier? Give two suggestions.
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. Grilled chicken with a salad. 2. Fish with vegetables and water instead of soda.

intitule: Writing: A Recipe Using Sequence Markers

enonce.texte:
Write a short recipe (5-6 sentences) for a healthy snack or meal. Use sequence markers: First, Then, After that, Finally.

Useful vocabulary: wash, cut, mix, cook, add, put, eat, enjoy

questions.texte: Write a recipe using sequence markers.
questions.reponse:
A good recipe should include:
- A title (e.g., How to Make a Fruit Smoothie)
- A list of ingredients (optional but helpful)
- At least 4 steps with sequence markers (First, Then, After that, Finally)
- Action verbs (wash, cut, mix, add, put, blend, eat)
- 5-6 sentences
- Correct grammar and spelling

enonce.texte:
Match each meal with the correct time of day.

Meals: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, Snack

Times: A. 7:00 am, B. 12:00 pm, C. 4:00 pm, D. 7:00 pm

questions.texte: Match each meal with its time.
questions.reponse:
1. Breakfast → A. 7:00 am
2. Lunch → B. 12:00 pm
3. Snack → C. 4:00 pm
4. Dinner → D. 7:00 pm

intitule: Fill in the Blanks: Sequence Markers

enonce.texte:
Complete the text with the correct sequence markers: First, Then, After that, Finally.

How to make a sandwich:

______, take two slices of bread. ______, put some butter on the bread. ______, add cheese and lettuce. ______, close the sandwich and enjoy!

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct sequence markers.
questions.reponse: First, take two slices of bread. Then, put some butter on the bread. After that, add cheese and lettuce. Finally, close the sandwich and enjoy!
